title: Fix example in `populate_by_name` documentation
body:
Fix the docstring of ConfigDict's populate_by_name.

Related issue number: fix #11765

Checklist:
- Unit tests exist
- Documentation reflects the changes where applicable

step_plan.summary: Correct a copy-paste error in ConfigDict.populate_by_name's docstring by changing the second example to use the attribute name (Model(my_field='foo')) so it properly demonstrates population by attribute name vs alias.

steps.operation: Fix example in populate_by_name docstring to demonstrate attribute-name population
steps.reason: The docstring example currently uses the alias-based instantiation twice (copy-paste error). Replace the second example's instantiation (Model(my_alias='foo')) with the attribute-name form (Model(my_field='foo')) so the example correctly demonstrates alias vs attribute-name population, consistent with the validate_by_name docstring.
steps.side_effects: This is a documentation-only change with no runtime behavior change. Run unit tests (at least tests/test_config.py) and ideally the full test suite to ensure no tests assert on docstrings. Documentation builds may need to be regenerated. No API or dependent-code changes expected; only consumers reading the docs will see the fix.
